key_risks: A hawkish FOMC statement or press conference could lift yields and trigger a reversal in high-beta technology, semiconductor, and momentum trades.
South Korean equities and semiconductor stocks are highly extended after very large trailing gains and could mean-revert sharply during the scoring week.
Silver could reverse if real yields or the US dollar rise after the Fed meeting, reducing precious-metals demand.
A broad risk-off move around retail sales, jobless claims, or post-FOMC positioning could make the concentrated high-volatility portfolio underperform SPY.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest short-term and medium-term momentum in the universe, with semiconductor/export leverage that could keep attracting performance-chasing flows over the one-week window.
Leading AI/technology subsector with very strong 7-day, 30-day, and 6-month trend; positioned to outperform if risk appetite and AI-chip leadership persist.
Broad US momentum exposure captures current winners while diversifying single-theme risk; recent performance suggests trend persistence into the shortened trading week.
Strongest precious-metals momentum with sensitivity to inflation concerns and potential dollar weakness, offering differentiated upside versus equities.
Solid recent momentum with less direct dependence on consumer data and a potential support from geopolitical and defense-spending themes.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio leans into the strongest observable one-week momentum while diversifying across Korea/semiconductors, US factor momentum, silver, and aerospace-defense. With only a one-week close-to-close horizon, trend persistence and liquidity are prioritized over valuation or long-term fundamentals.
rationale_summary: I expect high-momentum, semiconductor-linked and precious-metal exposures to have the best chance of beating the S&P 500 over the June 16 to June 23 window. The allocation intentionally accepts high volatility to maximize expected relative return.
